search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae allergic rhinitis telehealth guideline PDF
site:doh.gov.ae allergic rhinitis guideline Abu Dhabi
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Common-cold guidance does not establish allergic rhinitis.
population_applicability: Direct for adults and children presenting with allergic-rhinitis complaints, with medicine and age qualifiers not mapped.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth; partial for in-person nasal, throat, ear, or neck examination.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 document, effective 2024-04-21 and due for revision 2029-02-21, was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA allergic-rhinitis issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped allergic-rhinitis terminology, rhinorrhoea, sneezing, postnasal drip, smell, eye symptoms, triggers, timing, and selected nasal fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists sneezing, itching, rhinorrhoea, postnasal drip, congestion, smell change, eye symptoms, timing, and allergen or occupational triggers.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped epistaxis, fever, facial-pain, purulent-discharge, headache, unilateral, and visual warning fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists unilateral symptoms, visual change, epistaxis, fever, facial pain, purulent or bloody discharge, and systemic concerns.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-selected allergy-history or testing context without autonomous testing.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes clinician-selected specific-IgE testing by skin-prick or serum when confirmation is relevant.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-documented allergen, nasal-care, medicine-plan, return, question, and follow-up discussions without doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers clinician-led allergen education, management options, structural obstruction, non-response, imaging decisions, and referral criteria.

unresolved_source_gaps: Detailed nasal-mucosa, throat, ear, mastoid, hearing, tonsil, lymph-node, neck, shiner, facial-swelling, previous-record, fixed two-to-four-week, and combined history-draft fields remain unsupported.
Allergic rhinitis remains clinician-stated and symptoms must not be used to infer infection or structural disease.
Medicine choice or dose, autonomous treatment, referral urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
